question_text: What is the effect of unbalanced forces on an object?

options.label: A
options.text: The object always stays at rest

options.label: B
options.text: The object will change its speed or direction of motion

options.label: D
options.text: The forces cancel each other out

correct_answer: The object will change its speed or direction of motion
explanation: When forces on an object are unbalanced, there is a net force acting on it. This net force causes the object to accelerate, meaning it will either speed up, slow down, or change direction. This is a fundamental principle of Newton's second law of motion.
